bead.title: agent: add enforceable read-only tool reviewer profile
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The candidate-cycle design requires reviewers to inspect the live attempt worktree efficiently without being able to mutate it. Current reviewer dispatch is not enforced as read-only at the runtime boundary, so moving review into the attempt worktree would depend on prompt wording rather than a hard capability.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:623-673 builds reviewer AgentRunRuntime with Role=reviewer and MinPower metadata, but no read-only permission profile or tool restriction is set.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:826-842 sets implementation dispatch PermissionsOverride to unrestricted because implementation runs in an isolated worktree; reviewer dispatch needs the opposite boundary.
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go and related dispatch code are the likely boundary where PermissionsOverride / Role / tool access must be translated into Fizeau execution config.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an enforceable read-only tool reviewer profile to the agent runtime/dispatch boundary.
- Ensure reviewer role can read files and run read-only inspection commands if supported, but cannot write files, stage, commit, delete, or run mutating shell commands.
- Fail closed if a harness cannot enforce read-only reviewer mode, with an operator-visible review-error class rather than silently running unrestricted.
- Add tests with a fake reviewer that attempts mutation and verify the mutation is rejected or impossible.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not move review into the attempt worktree in this bead; that is a dependent bead.
- Do not implement repair cycles.
- Do not choose concrete provider/model fallback logic.

DEPS
Depends on the spec bead under this epic so the read-only tool reviewer contract is defined before implementation.
bead.acceptance:
1. A runtime/config field or permission profile exists for read-only tool reviewer dispatch and is wired through reviewer execution.
2. TestReviewerRuntime_ReadOnlyProfilePlumbed verifies BuildReviewExecuteRequest or its replacement sets the read-only reviewer profile for Role=reviewer.
3. TestReviewerRuntime_MutationDenied uses a fake or script reviewer that attempts to write/stage/commit and proves the attempt is rejected or cannot affect the worktree.
4. TestReviewerRuntime_UnsupportedReadOnlyFailsClosed verifies unsupported enforcement produces a typed review-error instead of unrestricted review.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestReviewerRuntime_ReadOnlyProfilePlumbed|TestReviewerRuntime_MutationDenied|TestReviewerRuntime_UnsupportedReadOnlyFailsClosed" green.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"rationale":"All 8 rubric criteria satisfied. (1) Title is imperative and names subsystem + change. (2) Description contains PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with three specific file:line anchors (execute_bead_review.go:623-673, execute_bead.go:826-842, service_run.go), PROPOSED FIX with enumerated steps, and NON-SCOPE. Minor hedge ('likely boundary') on service_run.go is acceptable given the proposed-fix section names it explicitly. (3) AC is numbered 1-7, each independently verifiable. (4) Three named Test* functions (TestReviewerRuntime_ReadOnlyProfilePlumbed, TestReviewerRuntime_MutationDenied, TestReviewerRuntime_UnsupportedReadOnlyFailsClosed) satisfy wired-in assertion requirement; AC 2 names a concrete function under test (BuildReviewExecuteRequest). (5) AC 6 is the go test gate and AC 7 is lefthook run pre-commit. (6) Labels carry phase:2, area:agent, kind:feature, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010. (7) Parent ddx-31e07fdc and dep ddx-71073116 are explicit; DEPS prose explains the ordering rationale. (8) Description is self-contained: no /tmp/ paths, no chat references, dep explanation inline.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}
